name: Olomana Three Peaks
tagline: Oahu's most thrilling ridge scramble — first peak is earned, second is gutsy, third is borderline reckless.
parking: Free parking at the loop on Royal Hawaiian Golf Club access road off Auloa Road in Kailua
bestTime: Start at sunrise to beat the heat — the exposed ridgeline gets brutally hot by midday
talkStory: Named after a legendary giant warrior who ruled windward Oahu until defeated and split apart by the chief Palila — the three peaks represent his severed body. The first peak (1,643 ft) is challenging but achievable; peaks two and three involve knife-edge ridges where multiple hikers have died from falls. Locals say never go when it's wet, always bring gloves for the rope sections, and turning back at peak one is the smart move — the views are already world-class.
